questions.text: The next 7 questions deal with the following situation: A person enters an elevator on the 4th floor and takes it up to the 10th floor. During this trip, what is the direction of their velocity vector?

answerChoices.text: upwards
downwards
The velocity is zero.

questions.text: When the elevator begins moving, what is the direction of the peron's acceleration vector?

answerChoices.text: upwards
downwards
The acceleration here is zero

questions.text: As the elevator moves, which statement best describes how the force of gravity on the person changes?

answerChoices.text: The force of gravity remains constant as the elevator moves.
When the person feels lighter, the force of gravity is weaker.
When the person feels heavier, the force of gravity is stronger.
When the person feels heavier, counterintuitively, the force of gravity is weaker.

questions.text: While the elevator is moving upwards at a constant speed, which statement best describes the rider's free-body diagram?

answerChoices.text: The magnitudes of the force of gravity and normal force are equal.
The normal force is stronger than the force of gravity.
The normal force is weaker than the force of gravity.
The normal force and the force of gravity are in the same direction.

questions.text: When the elevator reaches the 10th floor and begins to stop, what is the direction of the person's net force vector?

answerChoices.text: upwards
downwards
The net force is zero.

questions.text: When the elevator reaches the 10th floor and begins to stop, which statement best describes the rider's free-body diagram?

answerChoices.text: The magnitudes of the force of gravity and normal force are equal.
The normal force is stronger than the force of gravity.
The normal force is weaker than the force of gravity.
The normal force and the force of gravity are in the same direction.

questions.text: At the end of the motion of the elevator, the person feels like they are gently falling for a moment. What is the cause of this sensation?

answerChoices.text: The elevator overshot the 10th floor and needed to go back down.
For a short time, the floor is pressed less strongly into the rider's feet.
For a short time, the floor is pressed more strongly into the rider's feet.
The gravity in the elevator became stronger for a short time.
The gravity in the elevator became weaker for a short time.
The sensation is an illusion caused by being in a small, enclosed space that is not connected to the motion of the elevator.
